name: Cycas
description: [FILL IN] Cycas is the type genus of the family Cycadaceae and the only genus in that family. Cycas species are among the most ancient seed plants still living today, with a fossil record extending back to the Permian period. They are dioecious, palm-like gymnosperms native to tropical and subtropical regions across Africa, Asia, and the Pacific.
etymology: [FILL IN] The name Cycas derives from the Greek word 'kykas', used by Theophrastus to describe a palm-like tree.

conservation_status.notes: [FILL IN] Many species are listed as Threatened or Endangered on the IUCN Red List. C. revoluta is Least Concern in cultivation but threatened in the wild.

morphology.growth_form: [FILL IN] Palm-like perennial shrub or tree.
morphology.stem: [FILL IN] Stout, cylindrical trunk covered in persistent leaf-base scars; may be subterranean to several metres tall.
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] Pinnately compound, stiff, dark-green fronds arising in a crown; leaflets linear to lanceolate.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Female plants bear loose, leaf-like megasporophylls (no closed cone); male plants produce erect, compact pollen cones (strobili).
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Large, fleshy seeds; testa brightly coloured when ripe (red, orange, or yellow in many species).
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Taproot with lateral roots; specialised coralloid roots near the soil surface harbour nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria (Nostoc spp.).

ecology.pollination: [FILL IN] Primarily insect-pollinated (thrips and weevils in many species); some wind pollination also occurs.
ecology.seed_dispersal: [FILL IN] Seeds dispersed by vertebrates (birds, mammals, bats) attracted to the fleshy sarcotesta.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Obligate association with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria (Nostoc) in specialised coralloid roots.

uses.food: [FILL IN] Seeds and pith contain starch; processed to remove cycasin toxins for use as food in some cultures.
uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Cycas revoluta widely cultivated worldwide as an ornamental (sago palm).

cultivation.soil: [FILL IN] Well-drained, sandy or loamy soil; tolerates poor soils.
cultivation.light: [FILL IN] Full sun to partial shade.
cultivation.watering: [FILL IN] Drought-tolerant once established; avoid waterlogging.
cultivation.notes: [FILL IN] All parts are toxic if ingested; wear gloves when handling.
